name: Rebuff (Prompt Injection Defense)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Protect AI; API service is commercial

auth.notes: Rebuff API key for cloud service; or self-hosted

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-layer prompt injection detection: heuristics, LLM-based detection, vector similarity against known attacks. rebuff.detect_injection(input). Returns score and boolean. Log injection attempts to improve detection. Python and JavaScript SDKs. Self-hosted or Rebuff API. Protect AI company. Works as input validator before passing to LLM. Complements NeMo Guardrails (which focuses on output rails). Prompt injection is a growing concern for LLM apps. Microsoft Presidio covers PII; Rebuff covers injection.

gotchas: Project has low maintenance activity recently
False positive rate can be high for legitimate complex prompts
No perfect solution to prompt injection exists
Self-hosted requires Pinecone for vector store
best_when: LLM applications accepting untrusted user input
Agents with tool access where prompt injection could cause harm
Security-conscious LLM deployments needing injection detection layer
Applications where prompt injection could lead to data exfiltration
avoid_when: Internal LLM tools with trusted users
Production use requiring active maintenance (project may be stale)
summary: Rebuff detects prompt injection attacks via multi-layer defense: heuristics, LLM-based detection, and vector similarity against known attacks. detect_injection(input) returns risk score. Python and JS SDKs. Works as input validator before passing user input to LLM. Best for LLM applications accepting untrusted user input where prompt injection could cause harm. Project has limited recent maintenance — evaluate alternatives like LLM Guard for production use.
